description: Test your recall of key facts about the axilla, shoulder joint, and brachial plexus.

tip.tip: Think about how many walls it has — apex, base, and four walls.

cards.text: Which muscle divides the axillary artery into its three parts?

tip.tip: Lies deep to pectoralis major, attaches to coracoid process.

cards.text: How many branches does each part of the axillary artery give off?

tip.tip: The number matches the part number.

cards.text: Which spinal nerve roots are involved in Erb's palsy?

tip.tip: Upper trunk injury → waiter's tip position.

cards.text: What is the clinical presentation of Klumpke's paralysis?
Name the mnemonic for the four rotator cuff muscles.
Which rotator cuff muscle is most commonly torn?

tip.tip: Passes under the acromion, vulnerable to impingement.
Paralysis causes winging of the scapula.

cards.text: Which nerve wraps around the surgical neck of the humerus?

tip.tip: From the posterior cord (C5-C6), supplies deltoid.

cards.text: Name the five groups of axillary lymph nodes.
cards.answer: anterior, posterior, lateral, central, apical/pectoral, subscapular, lateral, central, apical
